title: Providing script input to the buildah container
body:
It would be nice to have a entrypoint or script baked into the official image that can interpret each line as buildah command and execute the same.

e.g. https://github.com/redhat-developer-demos/quarkus-java-builder/blob/master/bin/buildah.sh#L23-L55

The example above uses the maven build but that can be ignored. 

thoughts ?

comments.body:
(Tom ducks from @rhatdan's wrath).  We decided to not include an entry point in the image to allow it to be more flexible.  That way people could start up the container and then get into it and play around freely.  You of course could do a Dockerfile like:

FROM quay.io/buildah/stable:latest
ENTRYPOINT /usr/bin/buildah

and use that to create an image with an entrypoint set.  
As far as a script is concerned, do you have one in particular in mind?  We might be able to add something like that to the images.

Yes the use of a Entrypoint was seen as more of a pain then a benefit. I guess if  you want to add an Entrypoint, just download the stable image and do

```
ctr=$(buildah from quay.io/buildah/stable)
buildah config --entrypoint /usr/bin/buildah $ctr
buildah commit $ctr buildah
```
Now you should be ready to do execute:

```
podman run --device=/dev/fuse -v PATHTOCONTEXTDIR:/context:Z -v PATHTOSTORAGE:/var/lib/containers:Z buildah bud /context
```
I don't believe there's further work on this issue given the above discussions.  I'm closing it.  @kameshsampath thanks for the discussion, please open a new issue if you've further concerns.
